'''Liam Richard Finn''' (born 2 November 1983) is a professional [[rugby league]] coach who was the interim head coach of the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. He is a former {{rlnt|Ireland}} international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sh|so}} or {{rlp|hk}}.

==Background==
Finn was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire|Halifax]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.<ref name="halifaxcourier">{{cite web |title=Finn switches to the Wildcats |url=https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/finn-switches-to-the-wildcats-2562986 |website=Halifax Courier |access-date=6 June 2022 |date=22 October 2015}}</ref>

==Club career==
After brief stints in the [[Super League]] with [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] as a young player, Finn spent most of his career in the Championship, playing for [[Featherstone Rovers]] and the [[Dewsbury Rams]]. He returned to Super League in 2014 with the [[Castleford Tigers]], and played for them in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|publisher=Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

In October 2015, Finn re-joined Wakefield Trinity Wildcats on a two-year deal.<ref name="halifaxcourier" />

In September 2021, Finn announced that he would be retiring at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Dewsbury Rams veteran Liam Finn to retire admitting 'I couldn't give it my all anymore' |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/dewsbury-rams-veteran-liam-finn-to-retire-admitting-i-couldnt-give-it-my-all-anymore-3383192 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=5 June 2022 |date=14 September 2021}}</ref>

===Testimonial match===
A [[Benefit (sports)|benefit]] [[Benefit season|season]]/[[testimonial match]] for Liam Finn, allocated by the [[Rugby Football League]], took place at [[Featherstone Rovers]] during the 2013 season.

==International career==
Finn was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Cassidy included in Ireland squad">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in Ireland squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref><ref name="Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|title=Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=2008-08-01|access-date=2008-08-02|archive-date=14 April 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200414090730/http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2010 he represented Ireland in the [[Alitalia European Cup]]. Also he followed up his 2009 Championship 1 Player of the Year award with a Championship player of the year award in 2010 for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in his first season back with Rovers.

He was named as captain of Ireland in 2012, and was later confirmed as captain for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24388679|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013: Ireland name 24-man squad|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

He is Ireland's joint most capped player alongside [[Bob Beswick]] and is also Ireland's record point scorer.

In November 2014, Finn was called up to play for Ireland in their final [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|European Cup]] game against Wales. He was a huge influence scoring a total of 18 points in their sides massive 42–14 victory. However, their performance wasn't good enough as Ireland needed to win by 41 points if they were to secure the European Cup title, a place in the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]] and [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].

Unlike 2014, Finn was called up to the Irish squad in October before the [[2015 European Cup|European Cup]] which began on 17 October 2015.

In 2016 he was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|access-date=26 September 2016}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
===Dewsbury Rams===
Finn joined the [[Dewsbury Rams]] as their head coach in June 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://dewsburyrams.co.uk/2023/08/15/finn-to-depart-rams/ |title=Finn to depart Rams |website=Dewsbury Rams |date=15 August 2015 |access-date=26 December 2023}}</ref>

===Halifax Panthers===
In August 2023, Finn was appointed as head coach of Halifax Panthers on a three-year deal from the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62047/halifax-panthers-appoint-liam-finn-as-new-head-coach-from-2024 |title=Halifax Panthers appoint Liam Finn as new Head Coach from 2024 |website=Rugby-League.com |date=16 August 2023 |access-date=26 December 2023}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 26 September 2024, it was announced that he would take up an assistant coach role at [[Huddersfield Giants]] for 2025<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=26 Sep 2024|title=Liam Finn’s new Super League role announced following Halifax Panthers’ departure confirmation|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/liam-finn-super-league-role-announced-halifax-panthers-exit|author=Drew Darbyshire}}</ref>

Taken on the roll of interim coach of Huddersfield Giants from 28 March 2026 following departure of [[Luke Robinson (rugby league)|Luke Robinson]].
